result.finding: Minimal security implementation. No authentication, authorization, HTTPS/TLS, or encryption mechanisms are configured. The application runs as a plain HTTP service with no Spring Security dependency. Database credentials are externalized via environment variables. Input validation is present via Spring Validation (Bean Validation API).
evidence: No @EnableWebSecurity, @PreAuthorize, @Secured, JWT, or OAuth2 annotations found in Java source
No keystore or TLS certificate configuration found
No CORS, CSP, HSTS, or X-Frame-Options configuration found
No encryption (AES, RSA, Cipher) code found
spring-boot-starter-validation present for Bean Validation (input validation only)
MySQL password externalized as MYSQL_PASSWORD environment variable (not hardcoded)
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=* exposes all actuator endpoints without protection
values: Input Validation: Bean Validation API (@Valid annotations)
Secret Management: Environment variables for database credentials
Risk: Actuator endpoints fully exposed without authentication
